core_mechanism: Linear (eigenvalue) stability analysis of coarse-grained mean-field equations in which unresolved small-scale turbulent transport is represented by effective flux coefficients, so the growth rate of large-scale perturbations and the selected fastest-growing mode follow from a control parameter
brief_reason: The abstract's method is a generalized mean-field theory that parameterizes simulation-derived small-scale turbulent fluxes and then computes the stability of the system to large-scale perturbations, recovering several named instabilities as limiting cases with the fastest-growing mode set by the density ratio.
